lesson.menu_title: How Much? How Many? -- Quantifiers for the Seoul Trip
lesson.menu_desc: Quanto tempo, quantos documentos, dinheiro suficiente: os quantificadores (much, many, a little, a few, enough, too many) na chamada de logistica da delegacao para Seul -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- How Much? How Many?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 9 -- How Much? How Many?

listenings.text: Hi Maria Clara, it is Bruno. A quick update on the Seoul trip before you call me back. First, the time. We have a little time, about six weeks, but honestly there are too many meetings in October, so we need to start now. Second, the documents. We need a lot of documents for the delegation: passports, visas, and the invitation letters. A few visas are still not ready, so please talk to the embassy this week. Third, the money. We have enough budget for the flights, but not much foreign currency for the hotel and the meals, so we need to plan the amount carefully. And the seats. There are not many seats left on our flight, only eight, so we have to book them today. That is it. We do not have much time, but we have enough resources. Talk soon.

Good afternoon. This is a short message from the institutional travel office about the delegation to Seoul in November. Please read it before the next meeting. First, the documents. Every member of the delegation needs a valid passport and a visa. We need a lot of documents, so send them to us as soon as possible. We do not have much time. Second, the budget. Each member has a fixed amount for the trip. There is enough money for the flights and the hotel, but only a little for extra expenses, so please keep your receipts. Third, the seats. We have a limited number of seats on the official flight, so not many people can change the date. And finally, the preparation. There is a lot of preparation before departure, and too many changes make it difficult for everyone. Please confirm your information by Friday. Thank you.

items.situation: "How much time do we have before Seoul?"
tips: We have a little time, about six weeks.
We do not have much time, so we need to start now.

items.situation: "How many documents do we need for the delegation?"
tips: We need a lot of documents: passports, visas, and the letters.
We need a lot of documents, and a few visas are still not ready.

items.situation: "Do we have enough seats on the same flight?"
tips: There are not many seats left, only eight.
We do not have enough seats for everyone, so book them today.

items.situation: "Is there enough money for the whole trip?"
tips: We have enough budget for the flights, but only a little foreign currency.
There is enough money for the hotel, but not much for the extra expenses.

items.situation: "Are there too many meetings before November?"
tips: Yes, there are too many meetings in October.
We have too many meetings, and too much preparation for one week.

items.situation: Bruno asks if you are ready for Seoul. Answer, and use the expression from today.
tips: There is a lot of preparation, but I am looking forward to Seoul.
We have enough resources, and I am looking forward to the conference.
